sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT, lang VARCHAR(10), description VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_data VALUES (1, 'en', 'English description'), (2, 'fr', 'Description en français'), (3, NULL, 'No language specified');
SELECT DB.DBA.RDF_LANGMATCHES(lang, 'en') AS matches FROM test_data WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE lang IS NOT NULL);
SELECT created_at, COUNT(*) FROM test_data GROUP BY created_at HAVING COUNT(*) IN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data);
SELECT DB.DBA.RDF_LANGMATCHES(DB.DBA.RDF_LANGUAGE_OF_OBJ(description), 'fr') AS is_french FROM test_data WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE lang = 'fr');
SELECT DB.DBA.RDF_LANGMATCHES(lang, 'fr') AS matches FROM test_data WHERE NOT (id IN (SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE lang IS NULL));
SELECT DB.DBA.RDF_LANGMATCHES('es', lang) AS matches FROM test_data WHERE lang IN (SELECT lang FROM test_data WHERE description LIKE '%description%');
SELECT AVG(CASE WHEN DB.DBA.RDF_LANGMATCHES(lang, 'en') = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS english_percentage FROM test_data;
SELECT DB.DBA.RDF_LANGMATCHES(lang, 'en') AS lang_match, description FROM test_data WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day') ORDER BY lang_match DESC;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE DB.DBA.RDF_LANGMATCHES(lang, 'de') IN (0, 1);
